core_mechanism: first-order matrix-perturbation analysis of the eigendecomposition of a noisily estimated symmetric operator, propagating estimation noise into a null distribution for the movement of spectral projectors and eigenvalue functionals via a Davis-Kahan-type subspace-perturbation bound
brief_reason: The abstract derives a first-order null law for the change in the dominant-eigenspace projector between overlapping (correlated-noise) windows and uses a Davis-Kahan band plus bootstrap calibration to gauge how estimation noise perturbs the eigenspace and scalar spectral functionals, which is exactly first-order eigen-perturbation theory of a noisy symmetric matrix.
